articles.title: How Our DNA Has a Secret Code That Controls Genes
articles.summary: Our DNA has a secret code that helps decide which genes get turned on or off. DNA is made of tiny parts called codons that tell cells how to build proteins. Scientists used to think all codons were the same, but now they found that some are better at making proteins than others. A team from Japan discovered a protein called DHX29 that acts like a quality checker. It finds weak codons and stops them from making too many proteins. This helps cells work properly and might affect things like cancer. The research shows that DNA has more secrets than we thought, and scientists are excited to learn more about how this hidden code works in our bodies.

articles.title: Spices Team Up to Fight Body Swelling
articles.summary: Scientists found that mixing spices like chili peppers, mint, and eucalyptus can make them much better at reducing body swelling, called inflammation. Inflammation can cause diseases like diabetes and heart problems. The study showed that when capsaicin from chili peppers is combined with menthol from mint or 1,8-cineole from eucalyptus, it works 100 times better than using them alone. This happens because they use different pathways inside cells to calm down immune cells called macrophages. The research helps explain why eating a variety of plant-based foods in diets can be healthy. It could lead to new seasonings or supplements that use less spice but have stronger effects.

articles.title: Astronauts Come Home with Moon Treasures
articles.summary: Four astronauts are coming back to Earth after a trip around the Moon. They are in a spacecraft called Orion and will land in the ocean near San Diego. The astronauts took many pictures and have exciting stories to share about what they saw. They flew farther from Earth than any people before, breaking a record. They saw the far side of the Moon, which is the part we never see from Earth, and looked at its craters and plains. The crew talked to reporters from space and said they have lots of data and good stuff to bring home. They are happy to return and share their adventures with everyone.

articles.title: A Nature Reserve Helps Bring Back Tall Birds
articles.summary: A nature reserve in the UK is helping bring back common cranes, which are very tall birds. These birds went extinct in the UK about 400 years ago because people hunted them too much and their wetland homes disappeared. Now, the RSPB's Lakenheath Fen reserve in Suffolk has three pairs of cranes living there since 2007. Last year was a record year with 37 chicks born across the country, making about 250 cranes in total. The reserve has reedbeds that give the birds water and space away from people. The site manager, Dave Rogers, says it's a slow process to rebuild the population, but they are happy the birds chose their reserve. Cranes are over a meter tall and their calls can be heard from far away. Even though it's a good year, climate change might still be a problem for their homes.

articles.title: Ancient Humans in Australia: A DNA Discovery
articles.summary: Scientists have found that humans first arrived in Australia and New Guinea about 60,000 years ago. Long ago, during the Ice Age, these places were connected as one big land called Sahul. People have wondered for a long time when humans got there. Researchers from universities in the UK worked together to study DNA from people today, like Aboriginal Australians and New Guineans. They looked at a special type of DNA called mitochondrial DNA, which comes from mothers. By comparing DNA from different groups, they built a family tree that goes way back in time. The DNA showed that the oldest family lines started around 60,000 years ago. This means humans came earlier than some thought. The study also found that these early humans probably came from Southeast Asia, using different routes to reach Sahul. This helps us understand how people spread across the world long ago.

articles.title: A New Water Filter That Cleans Up Bad Chemicals
articles.summary: Scientists have made a special water filter that can clean up harmful chemicals called PFAS from water. These chemicals are found in groundwater and drinking water and can make people sick. The filter uses tiny cages that trap the PFAS, especially the short-chain ones that are hard to remove. In tests, it removed 98% of the chemicals and can be used many times. This could help make our drinking water safer for everyone.
